DISCOVERY 525, PLAINTIFF BRANDON HAUSAUER, MONICA MUZZIN MOTION TO HAVE MATTERS DEEMED ADMITTED AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SUBMITTED A SEPARATE STATEMENT OF FACTS BY WHICH THE COURT COULD DETERMINE THE ADEQUACY OF DEFENDANT'S RESPONSES. "[A]NY MOTION INVOLVING THE CONTENT OF A DISCOVERY REQUEST OR THE RESPONSES TO SUCH A REQUEST MUST BE ACCOMPANIED BY A SEPARATE STATEMENT." CAL. R. CT. 3.1345(A). THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT TOM@THOMASCOHEN.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: THOMAS COHEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (525/JPT)

DISCOVERY 525, PLAINTIFF BRANDON HAUSAUER, MONICA MUZZIN NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL FORM INTERROGARTORIES, AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHALL, WITHIN 10 DAYS, PROVIDE CODE-COMPLIANT RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION. AMONG OTHER THINGS, DEFENDANT HAS NOT SUBMITTED EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT ITS CLAIM OF MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE AND EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED TO PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,655. DISCOVERY 525, PLAINTIFFS BRANDON HAUSAUER, AND MONICA MUZZIN'S MOTION TO COMPEL TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHALL, WITHIN 10 DAYS, PRODUCE RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS WITHOUT OBJECTION. AMONG OTHER THINGS, DEFENDANT HAS NOT SUBMITTED EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT ITS CLAIM OF MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE AND EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED TO PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,985. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT TOM@THOMASCOHEN.COM. DISCOVERY, DEPT. 525, AS TO THE DEC-05-2024 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS BRANDON HAUSAUER, AND MONICA MUZZIN'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANT FCA US LLCS PERSON(S) MOST QUALIFIED AND DOCUMENT PRODUCTION, AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE MOTION IS GRANTED. THE DEPOSITION OF FCA'S PMQ AND THE PRODUCTION OF RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS TO NOS. 23-24, 29, 31-37, 39-45 SHALL PROCEED AS NOTICED BY PLAINTIFF. MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $4,185 ARE AWARDED TO PLAINTIFF AGAINST FCA AND/OR ITS COUNSEL. COUNSEL SHAWNA MELTON IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, AND EMAIL IT TO JUDGE PRO TEM TOM COHEN AT TOM@THOMASCOHEN.COM . Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendant Fca Us Llcs Person(S) Most Qualified And Document Production, And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-05-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. SHAWNA MELTON, Esq. O'Connor Law Group, P.C. (tel: 949-494-9090) for Plaintiffs.
Atty. MATTHEW SPOLSKY, Esq. of Clark Hill LLP (tel: 619-557-0404) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFFS BRANDON HAUSAUER, AND MONICA MUZZIN's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendant Fca Us Llcs Person(S) Most Qualified And Document Production, And Request For Monetary Sanctions.

Defendant contests the amount of sanctions. Both parties stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem and authorize the clerk to sign the stipulation on their behalf. Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The Motion is granted. The deposition of FCA's PMQ and the production of responsive documents to Nos. 23-24, 29, 31-37, 39-45 shall proceed as noticed by Plaintiff.
Monetary sanctions in the amount of $4,185 are awarded to Plaintiff against FCA and/or its counsel.

Counsel Shawna Melton is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, and email it to Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen at tom@thomascohen.com .

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. =(525/JPT)

Motion To Have Matters Deemed Admitted And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-07-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
MIRIAM ALKHALILI, ESQ. CLARK HILL LLP (619) 557-0404 Carkhill.fca@clarkhill.com Attorney for Defendant
FCA US LLC
No Other appearances

Proper notice of appearance was not provided to opposing counsel as well as to the court.

Counsel for defendant does not contest the tenative ruling but request the court to modify the amount of days defendnat is order to provide responses. Defendants request to have 30 days rather than 10 days to respond is denied by Court.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF BRANDON HAUSAUER, MONICA MUZZIN Motion To Have Matters Deemed Admitted And Request For Monetary Sanctions

The Motion is denied. Plaintiff has not submitted a separate statement of facts by which the court could determine the adequacy of Defendant's responses. "[A]ny motion involving the content of a discovery request or the responses to such a request must be accompanied by a separate statement." Cal. R. Ct. 3.1345(a).

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at tom@thomascohen.com

Judge Pro Tem: Thomas Cohen; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (525/JPT)

FCA US LLC

Counsel for defendant does not contest the tenative ruling but request the court to modify the amount of days defendnat is order to provide responses. Defendants request to have 30 days rather than 10 days to respond is denied by Court.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF BRANDON HAUSAUER, MONICA MUZZIN Notice Of Motion To Compel To Special Interrogatories, And Request For Sanctions

The Motion is granted. Defendant shall within 10 days, provide written, verified responses to Plaintiffs' Form Interrogatories, Set One without objections. Among other things, Defendant has not submitted evidence to support its claim of mistake, inadvertence and excusable neglect. Sanctions are awarded to Plaintiff in the amount of $1,655.
